buggy_code:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

df = pd.read_csv('returns.csv', parse_dates=['date'], index_col='date')

# Total cumulative return over entire series
total_cum_return = (1 + df['returns']).prod() - 1

# Normalize each return by total cumulative — uses full future series
df['normalized'] = df['returns'] / total_cum_return

# Use as feature
df['signal'] = (df['normalized'] > 0).astype(int)
clean_code:
import pandas as pd

df = pd.read_csv('returns.csv', parse_dates=['date'], index_col='date')

# Use expanding cumulative return — only uses past data
df['cum_return'] = (1 + df['returns']).expanding().apply(lambda x: x.prod() - 1)

# Normalize using expanding mean — no future data
df['normalized'] = df['returns'] / df['returns'].expanding().std()

df['signal'] = (df['normalized'].shift(1) > 0).astype(int)
description: Total cumulative return computed over full series used to normalize individual returns.
explanation: total_cum_return uses ALL returns including future ones. Dividing each row's return by this value means every row's feature depends on future data. The normalization factor at time T includes returns from T+1, T+2, etc.
suggested_fix: Use expanding() to compute cumulative statistics that only use data available up to each point in time.

issues.title: Cumulative return normalization uses full future series
